steps.description: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center presents options for the seven SLO behavioral descriptors at the admin meeting with the IHS Principal, the Director of AAO, the Director of Student Affairs Office, and the Heads of English, Math, Sciences, Humanities, Art Department, Business and Economics, and the Music and Sports Department. The existing Confident descriptor is tabled as the depth benchmark. Options cover authoring teams, validation route, publication channels in the staff handbook and parent handbook, and the unit-plan and gradebook tagging approach.
steps.assessment: The IHS Principal records a decision on authoring teams, validation route, and publication channels in the admin meeting minutes held by the Principal's Office (PO).

steps.description: The PD Leader and the Senior Teaching Coordinator finalize the initial draft of six SLO behavioral descriptors for Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible, each at the depth of the existing Confident descriptor. Drafting teams include the Head of English, Head of Humanities, Head of Math, Head of Sciences, Head of Art Department, Head of Business and Economics, and Homeroom Teachers from the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being). The draft also specifies the unit-plan SLO-naming field and the SLO-tagged assessment artifact field.
steps.assessment: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center signs off the initial draft document and files it in the Curriculum and Teaching shared store.

steps.description: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center publicizes the draft descriptors and the unit-plan and gradebook tagging fields to Staff / Faculty, Students, Parents, and the Parent Committee. Staff / Faculty review runs through Heads of Department in English, Math, Sciences, Humanities, Art Department, Business and Economics, the Music and Sports Department, and through the AS Year Leader, A2 Year Leader, IG1 Year Leader, and IG2 Year Leader for Homeroom Teachers. Student review runs through the Homeroom Teachers. Parent review runs through the Parent Committee. Administration / Leadership review runs through the IHS Principal and the Director of AAO.
steps.assessment: The Senior Teaching Coordinator logs feedback submissions from each Head of Department, each Year Leader, the Parent Committee, and the IHS Principal in a single tracking sheet held by Curriculum and Teaching.

steps.description: The PD Leader and the Senior Teaching Coordinator integrate feedback into the six descriptors and into the unit-plan and gradebook tagging specification. Each change is recorded against its source: Head of English, Head of Math, Head of Sciences, Head of Humanities, Head of Art Department, Head of Business and Economics, the Music and Sports Department teachers, the Year Leaders in SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being), the Parent Committee, the IHS Principal, and the Director of AAO. Conflicting feedback is resolved by the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center.
steps.assessment: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center reviews the revision log and the revised descriptors and confirms each feedback item is either integrated or recorded as declined with a reason.

steps.description: The IHS Principal validates the final descriptor set and the unit-plan and gradebook tagging specification. The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center publishes the seven descriptors in the staff handbook and the parent handbook. The Director of AAO publishes the descriptors in the student-facing learning materials maintained by the Academic Affairs Office. The Class Schedule Officer confirms PLC slots in the Class schedule framework that will carry the SLO-framed opening and SLO-tagged closing.
steps.assessment: The IHS Principal records validation in the Principal's Office (PO) minutes. The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center confirms the staff handbook, parent handbook, and student-facing materials carry the published descriptor set.

steps.description: The Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center propagates the descriptors into the unit-plan template and the gradebook used by every department. The PD Leader runs a working session for each Head of Department — English, Math, Sciences, Humanities, Art Department, Business and Economics — and for the Music and Sports Department teachers, the Librarian, the College Counseling Department Coordinator, and the Director of Future Scholar Innovation Center, so each unit plan for the 2025-2026 cycle names the one or two SLOs it advances and carries at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact. The Director of Student Affairs Office propagates the descriptors into Homeroom Teacher routines and into the term and portfolio student-reflection prompts. The IHS Assistant propagates the SLO-organized narrative section into the parent report template.
steps.assessment: The Senior Teaching Coordinator inspects the unit-plan repository and the gradebook for every department and confirms each unit plan names its advancing SLOs and links at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact. The Director of Student Affairs Office confirms the Homeroom Teacher reflection prompts and portfolio review prompts use the SLO vocabulary. The IHS Assistant confirms the parent report template carries the SLO-organized narrative section for each reporting cycle.
